content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Andreas Borgeas 
Borgeas for Senate 2018 
1625 E. Shaw, #130 
Fresno, CA 93710 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-222 
Dear Mr. Borgeas: 
November 8, 2018 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 
Also, please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby 
(1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. 
If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us 
for additional advice. 
QUESTION 
May your campaign committee pay for the travel, food and lodging costs for select 
campaign volunteers and supporters to attend your swearing in ceremony? 
CONCLUSION 
Your campaign committee may not pay for travel, food and lodging costs for supporters to 
attend your swearing in ceremony because such costs are not directly related to a political, 
legislative, or governmental purpose. However, you may use campaign funds to pay for the travel, 
food and lodging for campaign workers and staff to attend, this includes campaign volunteers, if the 
total value of the payments (including any other gifts provided to the individual in the same 
calendar year) is less than $250. 
FACTS 
At the time you submitted this request for advice you were a candidate for California State 
Senate District 8 and you have now prevailed in the election. You would like to know if your 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-18-222 
Page No. 2 
campaign committee may pay for the transportation, food and lodging for select volunteers and 
supporters who may or may not be campaign contributors to your campaign to attend your swearing 
in ceremony and whether any gift limits apply. 
ANALYSIS 
With regard to the use of campaign funds, all contributions deposited into the campaign 
account are required to be deemed to be held in trust for expenses associated with the election of the 
candidate or for expenses associated with holding office. (Section 89510.) The Act's provisions 
restricting the "personal use" of campaign funds are designed to prevent candidates, elected 
officials, and others who control the expenditures of campaign funds from benefiting privately from 
their campaign activities. The general rule is that an expenditure of campaign funds must be 
reasonably related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. A higher standard, requiring 
a "direct relationship" applies where an expenditure of campaign funds confers a substantial 
personal benefit on those with authority to approve the expenditure of campaign funds; or if the 
expenditure is for a particular class of activity. (Sections 89511 through 89518.) A particular class 
of activities requiring the higher, "directly related," standard -include travel and the making of 
personal gifts. (Section 89513.) 
Payment from Campaign Funds for Travel and Necessary Accommodations for Campaign 
Workers and Staff including Campaign Volunteers 
With regard to payments for travel, Section 89513(a)(l) provides that campaign funds shall 
not be used to pay or reimburse the candidate, the elected officer, or any individual or individuals 
with the authority to approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by a committee, or employees 
or staff of the committee, or the elected officer's governmental agency for travel expenses and 
necessary accommodations except when these expenditures are directly related to a political, 
legislative, or governmental purpose. 
Section 89513(±)(3) provides that "[a]n election victory celebration or similar campaign 
event, or gifts with a total cumulative value of less than ($250) in a single year made to an 
individual employee, a committee worker, or an employee of the elected officer's agency, are 
considered to be directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose." 
A gift is defined under the Act as any payment that confers a personal benefit on the 
recipient, to the extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not received. (Section 82028.) 
Section 89513(±)(1) permits the use of campaign funds for gifts only if there is a direct relationship 
between the expenditure for the gifts and a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. 
We have previously advised that a "thank you" event which would be held on the night of 
the election for the purpose of "thanking" campaign workers and supporters of the candidate is in 
the nature of an election victory celebration or similar campaign event and such an event would fall 
under the permissible use of campaign funds provision found in Section 89513(±)(3 ). (Murphy 
Advice Letter, No. A-92-116 and Demers Advice Letter, No. A-92-586.) We view the swearing-in 
ceremony, like a thank you event, as akin to an election victory celebration, and the Act provides 
that these types of events are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. 


File No. A-18-222 
Page No. 3 
However, the event itself is distinguishable from separate payments for travel, food, and 
lodging associated with the event. With respect to the travel, food ( other than food provided at the 
event) and lodging associated with attending the swearing in event for campaign workers and staff 
including campaign volunteers, these payments for an event after the election would constitute gifts 
to the workers, staff, or volunteer. Pursuant to Section 89513(£)(3), the payments are permissible 
only to the extent that the total cumulative value of the gifts provided to an individual employee, a 
committee worker, or an employee of the elected officer's agency is less than $250 for the calendar 
year. However, this is not the case for those "supporters" who are not staff or volunteers of the 
committee. 
Payment from Campaign Funds/or Travel and Necessary Accommodations/or Campaign 
"Sup porters" 
Expenditures related to travel, food and lodging for campaign "supporters" after the election 
are personal gifts that are not directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. 
Therefore, campaign funds may not be used to make payments for travel, food or lodging associated 
with attendance at the swearing in, though the supporters may attend the event itself and consume 
food provided at the event. 
Please note a committee controlled by a candidate is required to briefly describe the 
political, legislative, or governmental purpose for campaign expenditures.related to a gift, meal or 
travel on its campaign reports. Please refer to Regulation 18421. 7, which provides detailed 
requirements for reporting of these types of campaign expenditures. 
SKB:jgl 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
By: 
Sukhi K. Brar 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

calendar year) is less than $250.
